text: Sleep extension (increasing nighttime sleep duration) is the most effective sleep intervention for improving physical and cognitive performance in athletes.
Napping is one of the most effective and well-supported strategies to improve both sleep and subsequent athletic performance.
Sleep hygiene education alone does not appear to improve sleep or subsequent performance and recovery in athletes.
Removing electronic devices at night does not by itself improve sleep or subsequent athletic performance.
Cold water immersion does not improve sleep or subsequent performance and recovery in athletes.
Mindfulness interventions show promising but not yet confirmed effects on improving sleep and performance in athletes.
Light manipulation interventions show promising but not yet confirmed effects on improving sleep and performance in athletes.
